title: Information Retrieval & BM25
learning_outcomes: Formulate the Okapi BM25 ranking metric.
Explain term frequency saturation and document length normalization.

content_sections.title: Okapi BM25 Ranking Model
content_sections.content:
Okapi BM25 is a non-linear ranking function that improves on TF-IDF by incorporating term frequency saturation and document length normalization. The BM25 score for a document $D$ given query $Q$ containing search terms $q_i$ is:

$$\text{Score}(D, Q) = \sum_{q_i \in Q} \text{IDF}(q_i) \frac{f(q_i, D) \cdot (k_1 + 1)}{f(q_i, D) + k_1 \left( 1 - b + b \frac{|D|}{\text{avgdl}} \right)}$$

where:
*   $f(q_i, D)$ is the frequency of query term $q_i$ in document $D$.
*   $|D|$ and $\text{avgdl}$ are the document length and average document length across the corpus.
*   $k_1$ is a scaling parameter controlling term frequency saturation (typically $1.2 \le k_1 \le 2.0$).
*   $b$ is a scaling parameter controlling document length normalization (typically $b=0.75$).

content_sections.title: Term Saturation and Length Normalization Parameters
content_sections.content:
In TF-IDF, score growth is linear or log-linear with term frequency: many occurrences of a query term can heavily distort the ranking. In BM25, as term frequency increases, the term's contribution asymptotically approaches a limit controlled by $k_1$. 

The parameter $b$ controls length normalization. A longer document naturally contains more words, increasing the probability of matching query terms by chance. Setting $b=1$ applies full document length normalization, while $b=0$ disables it entirely.
